109→ext {
110→
111→    jvmVersion = Jvm.current().javaVersion.majorVersion
112→    if (jvmVersion != "17" && jvmVersion != "21") {
113→        println "\n\n\n"
114→        println "**************************************************************************************************************"
115→        println "\n\n\n"
116→        println "ERROR: AnkiDroid builds with JVM version 17 or 21."
117→        println "  Incompatible major version detected: '" + jvmVersion + "'"
118→        println "\n\n\n"
119→        println "  If you receive this error because you want to use a newer JDK, we may accept PRs to support new versions."
120→        println "  Edit the main build.gradle file, find this message in the file, and add support for the new version."
121→        println "  Please make sure the `jacocoTestReport` target works on an emulator with our minSdkVersion (currently 23)."
122→        println "\n\n\n"
123→        println "**************************************************************************************************************"
124→        println "\n\n\n"
125→        System.exit(1)
126→    }
127→    ciBuild = System.getenv("CI") == "true" // works for Travis CI or Github Actions
128→    // allows for -Dpre-dex=false to be set
129→    preDexEnabled = "true" == System.getProperty("pre-dex", "true")
130→    // allows for universal APKs to be generated
131→    universalApkEnabled = "true" == System.getProperty("universal-apk", "false")
132→
133→    if (System.getProperty("os.name") == "Mac OS X") {
134→        // macOS reports hardware cores. This is accurate for CI, Intel (halved due to SMT) and Apple Silicon
135→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = "sysctl -n hw.physicalcpu".execute().text.toInteger()
136→    } else if (ciBuild) {
137→        // GitHub Actions run on Standard_D4ads_v5 Azure Compute Units with 4 vCPUs
138→        // They appear to be 2:1 vCPU to CPU on Linux/Windows with two vCPU cores but with performance 1:1-similar
139→        // Sources to determine the correct Azure Compute Unit (and get CPU count) to tune this:
140→        // Which Azure compute unit in use? https://github.com/github/docs/blob/a25a33bb6cbf86a629d0a0c7bef624743991f97e/content/actions/using-github-hosted-runners/about-github-hosted-runners/about-github-hosted-runners.md?plain=1#L176
141→        // What is that compute unit? https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/dasv5-dadsv5-series#dadsv5-series
142→        // How does it perform? https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dadsv5 (vs previous Standard_DS2_v2 https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dv2---general-compute)
143→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = 4
144→    } else {
145→        // Use 50% of cores to account for SMT which doesn't help this workload
146→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = Runtime.runtime.availableProcessors().intdiv(2) ?: 1
147→    }
148→}

 55→        // The version number is of the form:

 57→        // The <build> is only present for alpha and beta releases (e.g., 2.0.4alpha2 or 2.0.4beta4), developer builds do
 58→        // not have a build number (e.g., 2.0.4dev) and official releases only have three components (e.g., 2.0.4).

 60→        // The version code is derived from the version name as follows:
 61→        // AbbCCtDD
 62→        // A: 1-digit decimal number representing the major version
 63→        // bb: 2-digit decimal number representing the minor version
 64→        // CC: 2-digit decimal number representing the maintenance version
 65→        // t: 1-digit decimal number representing the type of the build

174→    /**
175→     * Set this to true to create five separate APKs instead of one:
176→     *   - 2 APKs that only work on ARM/ARM64 devices
177→     *   - 2 APKs that only works on x86/x86_64 devices
178→     *   - a universal APK that works on all devices
179→     * The advantage is the size of most APKs is reduced by about 2.5MB.
180→     * Upload all the APKs to the Play Store and people will download
181→     * the correct one based on the CPU architecture of their device.
182→     */
183→    def enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture = true
184→
185→    splits {
186→        abi {
187→            reset()
188→            enable enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture
189→            //universalApk enableUniversalApk  // set in debug + release config blocks above
190→            include "armeabi-v7a", "x86", "arm64-v8a", "x86_64"
191→        }
192→    }
193→    // applicationVariants are e.g. debug, release
194→    applicationVariants.configureEach { variant ->
195→        // We want the same version stream for all ABIs in debug but for release we can split them
196→        if (variant.buildType.name == 'release') {
197→            variant.outputs.configureEach { output ->
198→
199→                // For each separate APK per architecture, set a unique version code as described here:
200→                // https://developer.android.com/studio/build/configure-apk-splits.html
201→                def versionCodes = ["armeabi-v7a": 1, "x86": 2, "arm64-v8a": 3, "x86_64": 4]
202→                def outputFile = output.outputFile
203→                if (outputFile != null && outputFile.name.endsWith('.apk')) {
204→                    def abi = output.getFilter("ABI")
205→                    if (abi != null) {  // null for the universal-debug, universal-release variants
206→                        //  From: https://developer.android.com/studio/publish/versioning#appversioning
207→                        //  "Warning: The greatest value Google Play allows for versionCode is 2100000000"
208→                        //  AnkiDroid versionCodes have a budget 8 digits (through AnkiDroid 9)
209→                        //  This style does ABI version code ranges with the 9th digit as 0-4.
210→                        //  This consumes ~20% of the version range space, w/50 years of versioning at our major-version pace
211→                        output.versionCodeOverride =
212→                                // ex:  321200106 = 3 * 100000000 + 21200106
213→                                versionCodes.get(abi) * 100000000 + defaultConfig.versionCode
214→                    }
215→                }
216→            }
217→        }
218→    }

260→// Issue 11078 - some emulators run, but run zero tests, and still report success
261→tasks.register('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') {
262→    doLast {
263→        // androidTest currently creates one .xml file per emulator with aggregate results in this dir
264→        File folder = file("./build/outputs/androidTest-results/connected/flavors/play")
265→        File[] listOfFiles = folder.listFiles({ d, f -> f ==~ /.*.xml/ } as FilenameFilter)
266→        for (File file : listOfFiles) {
267→            // The aggregate results file currently contains a line with this pattern holding test count
268→            String[] matches = file.readLines().findAll { it.contains('<testsuite') }
269→            if (matches.length != 1) {
270→                throw new GradleScriptException("Unable to determine count of tests executed for " + file.name + ". Regex pattern out of date?", null)
271→            }
272→            if (!(matches[0] ==~ /.* tests="\d+" .*/) || matches[0].contains('tests="0"')) {
273→                throw new GradleScriptException("androidTest executed 0 tests for " + file.name + " - Probably a bug with the emulator. Try another image.", null)
274→            }
275→        }
276→    }
277→}
278→afterEvaluate {
279→    tasks.named('connectedPlayDebugAndroidTest').configure { finalizedBy('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') }

262→    companion object {
263→        /**
264→         * Obtains the options for [AutomaticAnswer] in the deck config ("review" section)
265→         * @return null if the deck is dynamic (use global settings),
266→         * or if "useGeneralTimeoutSettings" is set
267→         */
268→        fun queryDeckSpecificOptions(
269→            action: AutomaticAnswerAction,
270→            col: Collection,
271→            selectedDid: DeckId
272→        ): AutomaticAnswerSettings? {
273→            // Dynamic don't have review options; attempt to get deck-specific auto-advance options
274→            // but be prepared to go with all default if it's a dynamic deck
275→            if (col.decks.isDyn(selectedDid)) {
276→                return null
277→            }
278→
279→            val revOptions = col.decks.confForDid(selectedDid).getJSONObject("rev")
280→
281→            if (revOptions.optBoolean("useGeneralTimeoutSettings", true)) {
282→                // we want to use the general settings, no need for per-deck settings
283→                return null
284→            }
285→
286→            val useTimer = revOptions.optBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
287→            val waitQuestionSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
288→            val waitAnswerSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
289→            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, useTimer, waitQuestionSecond, waitAnswerSecond)
290→        }

292→        fun queryFromPreferences(preferences: SharedPreferences, action: AutomaticAnswerAction): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
293→            val prefUseTimer: Boolean = preferences.getBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
294→            val prefWaitQuestionSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
295→            val prefWaitAnswerSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
296→            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, prefUseTimer, prefWaitQuestionSecond, prefWaitAnswerSecond)
297→        }
298→
299→        fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
300→            // deck specific options take precedence over general (preference-based) options.
301→            // the action can only be set via preferences (but is stored in the collection).
302→            val action = getAction(col)
303→            return queryDeckSpecificOptions(action, col, col.decks.selected()) ?: queryFromPreferences(preferences, action)

34→/**
35→ * A Card is the ultimate entity subject to review; it encapsulates the scheduling parameters (from which to derive
36→ * the next interval), the note it is derived from (from which field data is retrieved), its own ownership (which deck it
37→ * currently belongs to), and the retrieval of presentation elements (filled-in templates).
38→ *
39→ * Card presentation has two components: the question (front) side and the answer (back) side. The presentation of the
40→ * card is derived from the template of the card's Card Type. The Card Type is a component of the Note Type (see Models)
41→ * that this card is derived from.
42→ *
43→ * This class is responsible for:
44→ * - Storing and retrieving database entries that map to Cards in the Collection
45→ * - Providing the HTML representation of the Card's question and answer
46→ * - Recording the results of review (answer chosen, time taken, etc)
47→ *
48→ * It does not:
49→ * - Generate new cards (see Collection)
50→ * - Store the templates or the style sheet (see Models)
51→ *
52→ * Type: 0=new, 1=learning, 2=due
53→ * Queue: same as above, and:
54→ * -1=suspended, -2=user buried, -3=sched buried
55→ * Due is used differently for different queues.
56→ * - new queue: note id or random int
57→ * - rev queue: integer day
58→ * - lrn queue: integer timestamp
59→ */
60→open class Card : Cloneable {
61→    // Needed for tests
62→    var col: Collection
63→
64→    /**
65→     * Time in MS when timer was started
66→     */
67→    var timerStarted: Long
68→
69→    // Not in LibAnki. Record time spent reviewing in MS in order to restore when resuming.
70→    private var elapsedTime: Long = 0
71→
72→    // BEGIN SQL table entries
73→    @set:VisibleForTesting
74→    var id: Long
75→    var nid: NoteId = 0
76→    var did: DeckId = 0
77→    var ord = 0
78→    var mod: Long = 0
79→    var usn = 0
80→
81→    @get:CARD_TYPE
82→    @CARD_TYPE
83→    var type = 0
84→
85→    @get:CARD_QUEUE
86→    @CARD_QUEUE
87→    var queue = 0
88→    var due: Long = 0
89→    var ivl = 0
90→    var factor = 0
91→    var reps = 0
92→        private set
93→    var lapses = 0
94→    var left = 0
95→    var oDue: Long = 0
96→    var oDid: DeckId = 0
97→    private var customData: String = ""
98→    private var originalPosition: Int? = null
99→    private var flags = 0

27→/**
28→ * The options for playing sound for a given card
29→ *
30→ * @param replayQuestion deck option: "Skip question when replaying answer".
31→ * `true`: replay the question and the answer
32→ * `false`: only replay the answer
33→ * @param autoplay deck option: "Don't play audio automatically"
34→ */
35→class CardSoundConfig(val replayQuestion: Boolean, val autoplay: Boolean, val deckId: DeckId) {
36→    // PERF: technically, we can go further with options groups
37→    @CheckResult
38→    fun appliesTo(card: Card): Boolean = CardUtils.getDeckIdForCard(card) == deckId
39→
40→    companion object {
41→        @CheckResult
42→        fun create(collection: Collection, card: Card): CardSoundConfig {
43→            Timber.v("start loading SoundConfig")
44→            val deckConfig = collection.decks.confForDid(CardUtils.getDeckIdForCard(card))
45→
46→            val autoPlay = deckConfig.optBoolean("autoplay", false)
47→
48→            val replayQuestion: Boolean = deckConfig.optBoolean("replayq", true)
49→
50→            return CardSoundConfig(replayQuestion, autoPlay, card.did).apply {
51→                Timber.d("loaded SoundConfig: %s", this)
52→            }
53→        }
54→
55→        @CheckResult
56→        suspend fun create(card: Card): CardSoundConfig = withCol { create(this, card) }
57→    }
58→}

22→class Deck : JSONObject {
23→    /**

25→     *
26→     * This function will perform deepCopy on the passed object
27→     *
28→     * If you want to create a Deck without deepCopy
29→     * @see Deck.from
30→     */
31→    constructor(json: JSONObject) : super() {
32→        json.deepClonedInto(this)
33→    }
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Creates a deck object form a json string
37→     */
38→    constructor(json: String) : super(json)
39→
40→    /**
41→     * Creates a new empty deck object
42→     */
43→    private constructor() : super()
44→
45→    val isFiltered: Boolean
46→        get() = getInt("dyn") == Consts.DECK_DYN
47→
48→    val isNormal: Boolean
49→        get() = !isFiltered
50→
51→    var name: String
52→        get() = getString("name")
53→        set(value) {
54→            put("name", value)
55→        }

58→        get() = getBoolean("collapsed")
59→        set(value) {

61→        }
62→
63→    var id: DeckId
64→        get() = getLong("id")
65→        set(value) {
66→            put("id", value)
67→        }
68→
69→    var conf: Long
70→        get() {
71→            val value = optLong("conf")
72→            return if (value > 0) value else 1
73→        }

75→            put("conf", value)
76→        }

content.text:
The AnkiDroid backend has been updated to version 23.12, and the necessary changes have been implemented to support the new features and algorithm updates.

### Summary of Changes

1.  **Backend Version Update**:
    *   Updated `ankidroid_backend_version` in `build.gradle` to `0.1.34-anki23.12`.

2.  **Updated Export Logic**:
    *   Modified `Collection.exportAnkiPackage` in `BackendImportExport.kt` to use the new `exportAnkiPackageOptions` structure. This ensures that deck configurations are included in exports, as required by the 23.12 backend.

3.  **Filtered Deck Preview Delays**:
    *   Updated `FilteredDeckOptions.kt` to load and save separate preview delays for the **Again**, **Hard**, and **Good** buttons (`previewAgainSecs`, `previewHardSecs`, and `previewGoodSecs`). These options are now correctly persisted in the deck's configuration.

4.  **Auto-Advance Enhancements**:
    *   Updated `AutomaticAnswer.kt` to support the new Anki 23.12+ `autoAdvance` deck options. It now looks for `questionSeconds` and `answerSeconds` within the `autoAdvance` object in the deck configuration.
    *   Modified `AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` to re-initialize the `AutomaticAnswer` settings whenever the current card changes. This ensures that the auto-advance behavior correctly reflects the settings of the card's specific deck, rather than just the selected parent deck.

5.  **Improved Import Interface**:
    *   Updated `AnkiPackageImporterFragment.kt` to use the `import-anki-package.html` page instead of the legacy `import-page.html`. This is the recommended "proper fix" to enable the full range of import options available in the new backend.

### Implementation Details

*   **`FilteredDeckOptions.kt`**: Added logic to `cacheValues()` and `Editor.commit()` to handle the new preview delay keys.
*   **`AutomaticAnswer.kt`**: Added a `did` parameter to `createInstance` and updated `queryDeckSpecificOptions` to prioritize the new `autoAdvance` configuration over the legacy AnkiDroid-specific `rev` settings.
*   **`AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt`**: Added a call to `AutomaticAnswer.createInstance` within `updateCurrentCard()` to refresh settings on every card change.

These changes bring AnkiDroid in sync with the Anki 23.12 backend, ensuring consistent scheduling behavior and providing users with the latest features.

🤖 Generated with [Firebender](https://firebender.com)

Co-Authored-By: Firebender <help@firebender.com>

 24→ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 25→ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 26→ * and this routine will return false.
 27→ *
 28→ * There must not be an active transaction.
 29→ *
 30→ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 31→ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 32→ * to check for success.
 33→ *
 34→ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 35→ *
 36→ */
 37→fun Collection.createBackup(
 38→    backupFolder: String,
 39→    force: Boolean,
 40→    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 41→): Boolean {
 42→    return backend.createBackup(
 43→        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 44→        force = force,
 45→        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 46→    )
 47→}
 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 51→ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 52→ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 53→ */
 54→fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 55→    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
 56→}
 57→
 58→/**
 59→ * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 60→ * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 61→ * */
 62→fun importCollectionPackage(
 63→    backend: Backend,
 64→    colPath: String,
 65→    colpkgPath: String
 66→) {
 67→    backend.importCollectionPackage(
 68→        colPath = colPath,
 69→        backupPath = colpkgPath,
 70→        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
 71→        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
 72→    )
 73→}
 74→
 75→/**
 76→ * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 77→ * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 78→ * create, but older clients can not read it.
 79→ */
 80→fun Collection.exportCollectionPackage(
 81→    outPath: String,
 82→    includeMedia: Boolean,
 83→    legacy: Boolean = true
 84→) {
 85→    close(forFullSync = true)
 86→    backend.exportCollectionPackage(
 87→        outPath = outPath,
 88→        includeMedia = includeMedia,
 89→        legacy = legacy
 90→    )
 91→    reopen()
 92→}
 93→
 94→fun Collection.importAnkiPackageRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
 95→    return backend.importAnkiPackageRaw(input)
 96→}
 97→
 98→/**
 99→ * Export the specified deck to an .apkg file.
100→ * * If legacy is false, an apkg will be created that can only
101→ * be opened with recent Anki versions.
102→ */
103→fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
104→    outPath: String,
105→    withScheduling: Boolean,
106→    withMedia: Boolean,
107→    limit: ExportLimit,
108→    legacy: Boolean = true
109→) {
110→    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
111→}
112→
113→fun Collection.exportNotesCsv(
114→    outPath: String,
115→    withHtml: Boolean,
116→    withTags: Boolean,
117→    withDeck: Boolean,
118→    withNotetype: Boolean,
119→    withGuid: Boolean,
120→    limit: ExportLimit
121→) {
122→    backend.exportNoteCsv(outPath, withHtml, withTags, withDeck, withNotetype, withGuid, limit)
123→}
124→
125→fun Collection.exportCardsCsv(outPath: String, withHtml: Boolean, limit: ExportLimit) {
126→    backend.exportCardCsv(outPath, withHtml, limit)
127→}
128→
129→fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
130→    return backend.getCsvMetadataRaw(input)
131→}
132→
133→fun Collection.importCsvRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
134→    return backend.importCsvRaw(input)
135→}
136→

138→    return backend.buildSearchString(SearchNode.parseFrom(input))

14→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
15→
16→import android.content.Context
17→import android.content.Intent
18→import android.os.Bundle
19→import android.webkit.WebView
20→import androidx.activity.OnBackPressedCallback
21→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
23→import com.ichi2.anki.R
24→import com.ichi2.anki.SingleFragmentActivity
25→import com.ichi2.anki.hideShowButtonCss
26→
27→class AnkiPackageImporterFragment : PageFragment() {
28→    override val title: String
29→        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu_import)
30→    override val pageName: String
31→        get() = "import-page"
32→    override lateinit var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
33→    override var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient = PageChromeClient()
34→    private lateinit var backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
35→
36→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
37→        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
38→        backCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
39→            override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
40→                CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
41→                // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
42→                remove()
43→            }
44→        }
45→        val path = arguments?.getString(ARG_FILE_PATH)
46→            ?: throw IllegalStateException("No path provided for apkg package to import")
47→        webViewClient = AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(path, backCallback)
48→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
49→        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
50→    }
51→
52→    class AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(
53→        private val path: String,
54→        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
55→    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
56→        /**
57→         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
58→         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
59→         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
60→         */
61→        private var isDone = false
62→
63→        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
64→            val params = """{ type: "json_file", path: "$path"}"""
65→            // https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/main/ts/import-page/index.ts
66→            view!!.evaluateJavascript("anki.setupImportPage($params);$hideShowButtonCss;") {
67→                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
68→            }
69→        }
70→
71→        override fun onLoadResource(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
72→            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
73→            backCallback.isEnabled = when {
74→                url == null -> false
75→                url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
76→                url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
77→                    isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
78→                    false
79→                }
80→                else -> false
81→            }
82→        }
83→    }
84→
85→    companion object {
86→        private const val ARG_FILE_PATH = "arg_file_path"
87→
88→        fun getIntent(context: Context, filePath: String): Intent {
89→            val args = bundleOf(ARG_FILE_PATH to filePath)
90→            return SingleFragmentActivity.getIntent(context, AnkiPackageImporterFragment::class, args)
91→        }
92→    }
93→}

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 20→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 21→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.Reviewer
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.AnswerButtons.*

 29→import timber.log.Timber
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * AnkiDroid contains a setting: "Automatic display answer" which displays the
 33→ * question/answer automatically after a set period of time.
 34→ *
 35→ * ## Settings
 36→ * See: [AutomaticAnswerSettings]
 37→ *
 38→ * ## Implementation
 39→ *
 40→ * If "Enabled" is disabled, then nothing happens
 41→ * If Time to show Answer is 0, the automatic flipping code is disabled
 42→ * If Time to show next question is 0, the automatic answering code is disabled
 43→ *
 44→ * When a card is displayed (Front)
 45→ *
 46→ * If it has neither TTS nor audio, schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 47→ * If it has audio, calculate the time for the audio to play
 48→ *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" + "audio runtime" seconds
 49→ * If it has TTS, add a handler to the completion of TTS
 50→ *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 51→ *
 52→ * Do the same for the Back, but use the "Time to show next question" setting.
 53→ *
 54→ * If the "next question" is shown, "Again" is pressed.
 55→ *
 56→ * The implementation originally had multiple bugs regarding double presses of the answer buttons.
 57→ * This is because the user can press show the card at the same instant that the automatic code executes
 58→ *
 59→ * This has been worked around via the calls: [onSelectEase] and [onShowAnswer].
 60→ * Both of which clear the task queue for the question ([onSelectEase] or vice-versa)
 61→ */
 62→class AutomaticAnswer(
 63→    target: AutomaticallyAnswered,
 64→    @VisibleForTesting val settings: AutomaticAnswerSettings
 65→) {
 66→
 67→    /** Whether any tasks should be executed/scheduled.
 68→     *
 69→     * Ensures that auto answer does not occur if the reviewer is minimised
 70→     */
 71→    var isDisabled: Boolean = false
 72→        private set
 73→
 74→    /**
 75→     * Whether the sounds for the current side of the card have been played
 76→     * So [scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer] and [scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion] do nothing
 77→     * if called multiple times on the same side of the card
 78→     */
 79→    private var hasPlayedSounds: Boolean = false
 80→
 81→    private val showAnswerTask = Runnable {
 82→        if (isDisabled) {
 83→            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
 84→            return@Runnable
 85→        }
 86→        target.automaticShowAnswer()
 87→    }
 88→    private val showQuestionTask = Runnable {
 89→        if (isDisabled) {
 90→            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
 91→            return@Runnable
 92→        }
 93→        target.automaticShowQuestion(settings.answerAction)
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    /**
 97→     * Handler for the delay in auto showing question and/or answer
 98→     * One toggle for both question and answer, could set longer delay for auto next question
 99→     */
100→    @VisibleForTesting
101→    val timeoutHandler = HandlerUtils.newHandler()
102→
103→    @VisibleForTesting
104→    fun delayedShowQuestion(delay: Long) {
105→        if (isDisabled) {
106→            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
107→            return
108→        }
109→        Timber.i("Automatically showing question in %dms", delay)
110→        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showQuestionTask, delay)
111→    }
112→
113→    @VisibleForTesting
114→    fun delayedShowAnswer(delay: Long) {
115→        if (isDisabled) {
116→            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
117→            return
118→        }
119→        Timber.i("Automatically showing answer in %dms", delay)
120→        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showAnswerTask, delay)
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun stopShowQuestionTask() {
124→        Timber.i("stop: automatically show question")
125→        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showQuestionTask)
126→    }
127→
128→    private fun stopShowAnswerTask() {
129→        Timber.i("stop: automatically show answer")
130→        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showAnswerTask)
131→    }
132→
133→    fun enable() {
134→        isDisabled = false
135→    }
136→
137→    fun disable() {
138→        isDisabled = true
139→        stopShowAnswerTask()
140→        stopShowQuestionTask()
141→    }
142→
143→    /** Stop any "Automatic show answer" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
144→    fun onDisplayQuestion() {
145→        if (!settings.useTimer) return
146→        if (!settings.autoAdvanceAnswer) return
147→        hasPlayedSounds = false
148→
149→        stopShowAnswerTask()
150→    }
151→
152→    /** Stop any "Automatic show question" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
153→    fun onDisplayAnswer() {
154→        if (!settings.useTimer) return
155→        if (!settings.autoAdvanceQuestion) return
156→        hasPlayedSounds = false
157→
158→        stopShowQuestionTask()
159→    }
160→
161→    // region TODO: These attempt to stop a race condition between a manual answer and the automated answer
162→    // I don't believe this is thread-safe
163→
164→    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has selected an answer */
165→    fun onSelectEase() {
166→        Timber.v("onSelectEase")
167→        stopShowQuestionTask()
168→    }
169→
170→    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has flipped the card */
171→    fun onShowAnswer() {
172→        Timber.v("onShowAnswer")
173→        stopShowAnswerTask()
174→    }
175→
176→    // endregion
177→
178→    /**
179→     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowAnswer]
180→     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
181→     */
182→    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer(additionalDelay: Long = 0) {
183→        if (!settings.useTimer) return
184→        if (!settings.autoAdvanceAnswer) return
185→        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
186→        hasPlayedSounds = true
187→        delayedShowAnswer(settings.answerDelayMilliseconds + additionalDelay)
188→    }
189→
190→    /**
191→     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowQuestion]
192→     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
193→     */
194→    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion(additionalMediaDelay: Long = 0) {
195→        if (!settings.useTimer) return
196→        if (!settings.autoAdvanceQuestion) return
197→        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
198→        hasPlayedSounds = true
199→        delayedShowQuestion(settings.questionDelayMilliseconds + additionalMediaDelay)
200→    }
201→
202→    fun isEnabled(): Boolean {
203→        return settings.useTimer
204→    }
205→
206→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
207→    internal fun simulateCardFlip() {
208→        Timber.d("simulateCardFlip")
209→        hasPlayedSounds = false
210→    }
211→
212→    interface AutomaticallyAnswered {
213→        fun automaticShowAnswer()
214→        fun automaticShowQuestion(action: AutomaticAnswerAction)
215→    }
216→
217→    companion object {
218→        @CheckResult
219→        fun defaultInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered): AutomaticAnswer {
220→            return AutomaticAnswer(target, AutomaticAnswerSettings())
221→        }
222→
223→        @CheckResult
224→        fun createInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered, preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswer {
225→            val settings = AutomaticAnswerSettings.createInstance(preferences, col)
226→            return AutomaticAnswer(target, settings)
227→        }
228→    }
229→}
230→
231→/**
232→ * ### Reviewing - Automatic display answer
233→ *
234→ * * Enabled
235→ * * Time to show answer (20s)
236→ * * Time to show next question (60s)
237→ * * Automatic action: the action to take when reviewing
238→ *
239→ * ### Deck Options - Reviews - Automatic Display Answer
240→ *
241→ * These settings also exist in an options group
242→ * Although they're under "reviews", it counts for all cards
243→ *
244→ * * Use General settings (using the settings above - enabled by default)
245→ * * Enabled, etc...
246→ */
247→class AutomaticAnswerSettings(
248→    val answerAction: AutomaticAnswerAction = AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD,
249→    @get:JvmName("useTimer") val useTimer: Boolean = false,
250→    private val questionDelaySeconds: Int = 60,
251→    private val answerDelaySeconds: Int = 20
252→) {
253→
254→    val questionDelayMilliseconds = questionDelaySeconds * 1000L
255→    val answerDelayMilliseconds = answerDelaySeconds * 1000L
256→
257→    // a wait of zero means auto-advance is disabled
258→    val autoAdvanceAnswer; get() = answerDelaySeconds > 0
259→    val autoAdvanceQuestion; get() = questionDelaySeconds > 0
260→
261→    companion object {
262→        /**
263→         * Obtains the options for [AutomaticAnswer] in the deck config ("review" section)
264→         * @return null if the deck is dynamic (use global settings),
265→         * or if "useGeneralTimeoutSettings" is set
266→         */
267→        fun queryDeckSpecificOptions(
268→            action: AutomaticAnswerAction,
269→            col: Collection,
270→            selectedDid: DeckId
271→        ): AutomaticAnswerSettings? {
272→            // Dynamic don't have review options; attempt to get deck-specific auto-advance options
273→            // but be prepared to go with all default if it's a dynamic deck
274→            if (col.decks.isDyn(selectedDid)) {
275→                return null
276→            }
277→
278→            val revOptions = col.decks.confForDid(selectedDid).getJSONObject("rev")
279→
280→            if (revOptions.optBoolean("useGeneralTimeoutSettings", true)) {
281→                // we want to use the general settings, no need for per-deck settings
282→                return null
283→            }
284→
285→            val useTimer = revOptions.optBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
286→            val waitQuestionSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
287→            val waitAnswerSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
288→            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, useTimer, waitQuestionSecond, waitAnswerSecond)
289→        }
290→
291→        fun queryFromPreferences(preferences: SharedPreferences, action: AutomaticAnswerAction): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
292→            val prefUseTimer: Boolean = preferences.getBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
293→            val prefWaitQuestionSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
294→            val prefWaitAnswerSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
295→            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, prefUseTimer, prefWaitQuestionSecond, prefWaitAnswerSecond)
296→        }
297→
298→        fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
299→            // deck specific options take precedence over general (preference-based) options.
300→            // the action can only be set via preferences (but is stored in the collection).
301→            val action = getAction(col)
302→            return queryDeckSpecificOptions(action, col, col.decks.selected()) ?: queryFromPreferences(preferences, action)
303→        }
304→
305→        private fun getAction(col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerAction {
306→            return try {
307→                val value: Int = col.config.get(AutomaticAnswerAction.CONFIG_KEY) ?: return AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
308→                AutomaticAnswerAction.fromPreferenceValue(value)
309→            } catch (e: Exception) {
310→                AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
311→            }
312→        }
313→    }
314→}
315→
316→/**
317→ * Represents a value from [R.array.automatic_answer_values]/[R.array.automatic_answer_options]
318→ * Executed when answering a card (showing the question).
319→ */
320→enum class AutomaticAnswerAction(private val preferenceValue: Int) {
321→    /** Default: least invasive action */
322→    BURY_CARD(0),
323→    ANSWER_AGAIN(1),
324→    ANSWER_HARD(2),
325→    ANSWER_GOOD(3),
326→    ANSWER_EASY(4);
327→
328→    fun execute(reviewer: Reviewer) {
329→        val numberOfButtons = 4
330→        val actualAction = handleInvalidButtons(numberOfButtons)
331→        val action = actualAction.toCommand(numberOfButtons)
332→        Timber.i("Executing %s", action)
333→        reviewer.executeCommand(action)
334→    }
335→
336→    /** Handle **Hard/Easy** uf they don't appear */
337→    private fun handleInvalidButtons(numberOfButtons: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
338→        return when (this) {
339→            ANSWER_HARD -> if (AnswerButtons.canAnswerHard(numberOfButtons)) ANSWER_HARD else ANSWER_GOOD
340→            ANSWER_EASY -> if (AnswerButtons.canAnswerEasy(numberOfButtons)) ANSWER_EASY else ANSWER_GOOD
341→            // Again and Good always appear. So does Bury
342→            else -> this
343→        }
344→    }
345→
346→    /** Convert to a [ViewerCommand] */
347→    private fun toCommand(numberOfButtons: Int): ViewerCommand {
348→        return when (this) {
349→            BURY_CARD -> ViewerCommand.BURY_CARD
350→            ANSWER_AGAIN -> AGAIN.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
351→            ANSWER_HARD -> HARD.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
352→            ANSWER_GOOD -> GOOD.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
353→            ANSWER_EASY -> EASY.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
354→        }
355→    }
356→
357→    companion object {
358→        /**
359→         * An integer representing the action when Automatic Answer flips a card from answer to question
360→         *
361→         * 0 represents "bury", 1-4 represents the named buttons
362→         *
363→         * Although AnkiMobile and AnkiDroid have the feature, this config key is currently AnkiDroid only
364→         *
365→         * @see AutomaticAnswerAction
366→         */
367→        const val CONFIG_KEY = "automaticAnswerAction"
368→
369→        /** convert from [R.array.automatic_answer_values] ([R.array.automatic_answer_options]) to the enum */
370→        fun fromPreferenceValue(i: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
371→            return values().firstOrNull { it.preferenceValue == i } ?: BURY_CARD
372→        }
373→    }
374→}

30→suspend fun importJsonFileRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
31→    return withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
32→        val output = withCol { this.importAnkiPackageRaw(input) }
33→        val changes = OpChangesOnly.parseFrom(output)
34→        undoableOp { changes }
35→        output
36→    }

39→suspend fun importCsvRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
40→    return withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
41→        val output = withCol { importCsvRaw(input) }
42→        val changes = OpChangesOnly.parseFrom(output)
43→        undoableOp { changes }
44→        output
45→    }
46→}

48→/**
49→ * Css to hide the "Show" button from the final backend import page. As the user could import a lot
50→ * of notes, on pressing "Show" the native CardBrowser would be called with a search query
51→ * comprising of all the notes ids. This would result in a crash or very slow behavior in the
52→ * CardBrowser.
53→ *
54→ * NOTE: this should be used only with [android.webkit.WebView.evaluateJavascript].
55→ */
56→val hideShowButtonCss = """
57→    javascript:(
58→        function() {
59→            var hideShowButtonStyle = '.desktop-only { display: none !important; }';
60→            var newStyle = document.createElement('style');                    
61→            newStyle.appendChild(document.createTextNode(hideShowButtonStyle));
62→            document.head.appendChild(newStyle);       
63→        }
64→    )()
65→""".trimIndent()
66→
67→/**
68→ * Calls the native [CardBrowser] to display the results of the search query constructed from the
69→ * input. This method will always return the received input.
70→ */
71→suspend fun FragmentActivity.searchInBrowser(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
72→    val searchString = withCol { buildSearchString(input) }
73→    val starterIntent = Intent(this, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
74→        putExtra("search_query", searchString)
75→        putExtra("all_decks", true)
76→    }
77→    startActivity(starterIntent)
78→    return input
79→}

18→import android.content.Context
19→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
20→import androidx.webkit.WebViewAssetLoader
21→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
22→import timber.log.Timber
23→import java.io.File
24→import java.io.FileInputStream
25→
26→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
27→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
28→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
29→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
30→        .setDomain(domain)
31→        .addPathHandler("/web/") { path: String ->
32→            val inputStream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream("web/$path")
33→            WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
34→        }
35→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
36→            try {
37→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40→            } catch (e: Exception) {
41→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42→                null
43→            }
44→        }
45→        .build()
46→}

 18→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19→
 20→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25→
 26→const val PORT = 0
 27→// const val PORT = 40001
 28→
 29→// local debugging:
 30→// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31→
 32→open class AnkiServer(
 33→    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34→) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35→
 36→    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37→        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41→    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42→    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43→
 44→    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45→        val uri = session.uri
 46→        val mime = getMimeFromUri(uri)
 47→
 48→        if (session.method == Method.GET) {
 49→            val resourcePath = "web$uri"
 50→            val stream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream(resourcePath)
 51→            Timber.d("GET: Requested %s (%s), found? %b", uri, resourcePath, stream != null)
 52→            return newChunkedResponse(Response.Status.OK, mime, stream)
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        if (session.method == Method.POST) {
 56→            Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 57→            val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 58→            return buildResponse {
 59→                postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→        return newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun buildResponse(
 66→        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 67→    ): Response {
 68→        return try {
 69→            val data = runBlocking {
 70→                block()
 71→            }
 72→            newChunkedResponse(data)
 73→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 74→            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 75→        }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    companion object {
 79→        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 80→
 81→        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 82→        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 83→        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 84→
 85→        fun getMimeFromUri(uri: String): String {
 86→            return when (uri.substringAfterLast(".")) {
 87→                "ico" -> "image/x-icon"
 88→                "css" -> "text/css"
 89→                "js" -> "text/javascript"
 90→                "html" -> "text/html"
 91→                else -> "application/binary"
 92→            }
 93→        }
 94→
 95→        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 96→            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 97→            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 98→            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 99→            return bytes
100→        }
101→
102→        fun newChunkedResponse(
103→            data: ByteArray?,
104→            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
105→            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
106→        ): Response {
107→            return if (data == null) {
108→                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
109→            } else {
110→                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
111→            }
112→        }
113→    }
114→}

575→        // Use android.net.Uri class to ensure whole path is properly encoded
576→        // File.toURL() does not work here, and URLEncoder class is not directly usable
577→        // with existing slashes

16→package com.ichi2.anki.pages

18→import android.os.Bundle
19→import android.view.LayoutInflater
20→import android.view.View
21→import android.view.ViewGroup
22→import android.webkit.WebView
23→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
24→import com.google.android.material.appbar.MaterialToolbar
25→import com.ichi2.anki.R
26→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
27→import timber.log.Timber
28→
29→/**
30→ * Base class for displaying Anki HTML pages
31→ */
32→@Suppress("LeakingThis")
33→abstract class PageFragment : Fragment(), PostRequestHandler {
34→    abstract val title: String
35→    abstract val pageName: String
36→    abstract var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
37→    abstract var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient
38→
39→    lateinit var webView: WebView
40→    private val server = AnkiServer(this).also { it.start() }
41→
42→    override fun onCreateView(
43→        inflater: LayoutInflater,
44→        container: ViewGroup?,
45→        savedInstanceState: Bundle?
46→    ): View? {
47→        val view = inflater.inflate(R.layout.page_fragment, container, false)
48→
49→        webView = view.findViewById<WebView>(R.id.webview).apply {
50→            with(settings) {
51→                javaScriptEnabled = true
52→                displayZoomControls = false
53→                builtInZoomControls = true
54→                setSupportZoom(true)
55→            }
56→            webViewClient = this@PageFragment.webViewClient
57→            webChromeClient = this@PageFragment.webChromeClient
58→        }
59→        val nightMode = if (Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode) "#night" else ""
60→        val url = server.baseUrl() + "$pageName.html$nightMode"
61→
62→        Timber.i("Loading $url")
63→        webView.loadUrl(url)
64→
65→        view.findViewById<MaterialToolbar>(R.id.toolbar).apply {
66→            title = this@PageFragment.title
67→            setNavigationOnClickListener {
68→                requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
69→            }
70→        }
71→        return view
72→    }
73→
74→    override suspend fun handlePostRequest(uri: String, bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
75→        val methodName = if (uri.startsWith(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX)) {
76→            uri.substring(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX.length)
77→        } else {
78→            throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $uri")
79→        }
80→        return requireActivity().handleUiPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
81→            ?: handleCollectionPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
82→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled method: $methodName")
83→    }
84→}

 26→package com.ichi2.libanki
 27→
 28→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithCount
 29→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithId
 30→import anki.decks.FilteredDeckForUpdate
 31→import com.google.protobuf.kotlin.toByteStringUtf8
 32→import com.ichi2.libanki.backend.BackendUtils
 33→import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.*
 34→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.jsonObjectIterable
 36→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.RustCleanup
 37→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.exceptions.BackendDeckIsFilteredException
 38→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.exceptions.BackendNotFoundException
 39→import org.json.JSONArray
 40→import java.util.*
 41→
 42→data class DeckNameId(val name: String, val id: DeckId)
 43→
 44→// TODO: col was a weakref
 45→
 46→class Decks(private val col: Collection) {
 47→    /** "Add a deck with NAME. Reuse deck if already exists. Return id as int." */
 48→    fun id(name: String): DeckId {
 49→        val id = this.idForName(name)
 50→        if (id != null) {
 51→            return id
 52→        }
 53→        val deck = this.newDeckLegacy(false)
 54→        deck.name = name
 55→        addDeckLegacy(deck)
 56→        return deck.id
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    private fun addDeckLegacy(deck: Deck): OpChangesWithId {
 60→        val changes = col.backend.addDeckLegacy(
 61→            json = BackendUtils.to_json_bytes(deck)
 62→        )
 63→        deck.id = changes.id
 64→        return changes
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    fun removeDecks(deckIds: Iterable<Long>): OpChangesWithCount {
 68→        return col.backend.removeDecks(dids = deckIds)
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    /** A sorted sequence of deck names and IDs. */
 72→    fun allNamesAndIds(
 73→        skipEmptyDefault: Boolean = false,
 74→        includeFiltered: Boolean = true
 75→    ): List<DeckNameId> {
 76→        return col.backend.getDeckNames(skipEmptyDefault = skipEmptyDefault, includeFiltered = includeFiltered).map { entry ->
 77→            DeckNameId(entry.name, entry.id)
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    fun idForName(name: String): DeckId? {
 82→        return try {
 83→            col.backend.getDeckIdByName(name)
 84→        } catch (ex: BackendNotFoundException) {
 85→            null
 86→        }
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    fun get(did: DeckId): Deck? {
 90→        return try {
 91→            Deck(BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.getDeckLegacy(did)))
 92→        } catch (ex: BackendNotFoundException) {
 93→            null
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    private fun newDeckLegacy(filtered: Boolean): Deck {
 98→        val deck = BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.newDeckLegacy(filtered))
 99→        return Deck(
100→            if (filtered) {
101→                // until migrating to the dedicated method for creating filtered decks,
102→                // we need to ensure the default config matches legacy expectations
103→                val terms = deck.getJSONArray("terms").getJSONArray(0)
104→                terms.put(0, "")
105→                terms.put(2, 0)
106→                deck.put("terms", JSONArray(listOf(terms)))
107→                deck.put("browserCollapsed", false)
108→                deck.put("collapsed", false)
109→                deck
110→            } else {
111→                deck
112→            }
113→        )
114→    }
115→
116→    fun collapse(did: DeckId) {
117→        val deck = this.get(did) ?: return
118→        deck.collapsed = !deck.collapsed
119→        this.save(deck)
120→    }
121→
122→    fun count(): Int {
123→        return len(this.allNamesAndIds())
124→    }
125→
126→    /** Get deck with NAME, ignoring case. */
127→    fun byName(name: String): Deck? {
128→        val id = this.idForName(name)
129→        if (id != null) {
130→            return get(id)
131→        }
132→        return null
133→    }
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Add or update an existing deck. Used for syncing and merging.
137→     * @throws BackendDeckIsFilteredException
138→     */
139→    fun save(g: Deck) {
140→        g.id = col.backend.addOrUpdateDeckLegacy(
141→            BackendUtils.toByteString(g),
142→            preserveUsnAndMtime = false
143→        )
144→    }
145→
146→    /** Rename deck prefix to NAME if not exists. Updates children. */
147→    fun rename(g: Deck, newName: String) {
148→        g.name = newName
149→        this.save(g)
150→    }
151→
152→    /* Deck configurations */
153→
154→    /** A list of all deck config. */
155→    fun allConfig(): List<DeckConfig> {
156→        return BackendUtils.jsonToArray(col.backend.allDeckConfigLegacy())
157→            .jsonObjectIterable()
158→            .map { obj -> DeckConfig(obj) }
159→            .toList()
160→    }
161→
162→    /** Falls back on default config if deck or config missing */
163→    fun confForDid(did: DeckId): DeckConfig {
164→        val conf = get(did)?.conf ?: 1
165→        return DeckConfig(BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.getDeckConfigLegacy(conf)))
166→    }
167→
168→    fun save(g: DeckConfig) {
169→        g.id = col.backend.addOrUpdateDeckConfigLegacy(g.toString().toByteStringUtf8())
170→    }
171→
172→    private fun addConfig(
173→        name: String
174→    ): DeckConfig {
175→        val conf = DeckConfig(newDeckConfigLegacy())
176→        conf.name = name
177→        this.save(conf)
178→        return conf
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun newDeckConfigLegacy(): DeckConfig {
182→        return DeckConfig(BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.newDeckConfigLegacy()))
183→    }
184→
185→    fun setConf(grp: Deck, id: DeckConfigId) {
186→        grp.conf = id
187→        this.save(grp)
188→    }
189→
190→    /* Reverts to default if provided id missing */
191→    fun getConf(confId: DeckConfigId): DeckConfig =
192→        DeckConfig(BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.getDeckConfigLegacy(confId)))
193→
194→    fun confId(name: String): Long {
195→        return addConfig(name).id
196→    }
197→
198→    /* Deck selection */
199→
200→    /** The currently active dids. */
201→    @RustCleanup("Probably better as a queue")
202→    fun active(): LinkedList<DeckId> {
203→        val activeDecks = col.config.get<List<DeckId>>(ACTIVE_DECKS) ?: listOf()
204→        val result = LinkedList<Long>()
205→        result.addAll(activeDecks.asIterable())
206→        return result
207→    }
208→
209→    /** The currently selected did. */
210→    fun selected(): DeckId {
211→        return this.col.backend.getCurrentDeck().id
212→    }
213→
214→    fun current(): Deck {
215→        return this.get(this.selected()) ?: this.get(1)!!
216→    }
217→
218→    /** Select a new branch. */
219→    fun select(did: DeckId) {
220→        col.backend.setCurrentDeck(did)
221→        val selectedDeckName = name(did)
222→        val childrenDids =
223→            allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = false)
224→                .filter { it.name.startsWith("$selectedDeckName::") }
225→                .map { it.id }
226→        col.config.set(ACTIVE_DECKS, listOf(did) + childrenDids)
227→    }
228→
229→    /*
230→     Dynamic decks
231→     */
232→
233→    /** Return a new dynamic deck and set it as the current deck. */
234→    fun newDyn(name: String): DeckId {
235→        val deck = this.newDeckLegacy(true)
236→        deck.name = name
237→        addDeckLegacy(deck)
238→        this.select(deck.id)
239→        return deck.id
240→    }
241→
242→    fun isDyn(did: DeckId): Boolean {
243→        return this.get(did)?.isFiltered == true
244→    }
245→
246→    fun name(did: DeckId): String = get(did)?.name ?: "[no deck]"
247→
248→    /*
249→     * ***********************************************************
250→     * The methods below are not in LibAnki.
251→     * ***********************************************************
252→     */
253→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to extension method (possibly in servicelayer)")
254→    fun getActualDescription(): String = current().optString("desc", "")
255→
256→    /** @return the fully qualified name of the subdeck, or null if unavailable */
257→    fun getSubdeckName(did: DeckId, subdeckName: String?): String? {
258→        if (subdeckName.isNullOrEmpty()) {
259→            return null
260→        }
261→        val newName = subdeckName.replace("\"".toRegex(), "")
262→        if (newName.isEmpty()) {
263→            return null
264→        }
265→        val deck = get(did) ?: return null
266→        return deck.getString("name") + DECK_SEPARATOR + subdeckName
267→    }
268→
269→    /*
270→     * Not in libAnki
271→     */
272→
273→    companion object {
274→        /* Parents/children */
275→
276→        fun path(name: String): List<String> {
277→            return name.split("::")
278→        }
279→
280→        fun basename(name: String): String {
281→            return path(name).last()
282→        }
283→
284→        /** Invalid id, represents an id on an unfound deck  */
285→        const val NOT_FOUND_DECK_ID = -1L
286→
287→        /** Configuration saving the current deck  */
288→        const val CURRENT_DECK = "curDeck"
289→
290→        /** Configuration saving the set of active decks (i.e. current decks and its descendants)  */
291→        const val ACTIVE_DECKS = "activeDecks"
292→
293→        // not in libAnki
294→        const val DECK_SEPARATOR = "::"
295→
296→    /*
297→    * ***********************************************************
298→    * The methods below are not in LibAnki.
299→    * ***********************************************************
300→    */
301→        fun isValidDeckName(deckName: String): Boolean = deckName.trim { it <= ' ' }.isNotEmpty()
302→    }
303→}
304→
305→// These take and return bytes that the frontend TypeScript code will encode/decode.
306→fun Collection.getDeckNamesRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
307→    return backend.getDeckNamesRaw(input)
308→}
309→
310→/**
311→ * Gets the filtered deck with given [did]
312→ * or creates a new one if [did] = 0
313→ */
314→fun Collection.getOrCreateFilteredDeck(did: DeckId): FilteredDeckForUpdate {
315→    return backend.getOrCreateFilteredDeck(did = did)
316→}

14→ You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
15→ this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
16→ */
17→package com.ichi2.libanki
18→
19→import com.ichi2.utils.deepClonedInto
20→import org.json.JSONObject
21→import timber.log.Timber
22→
23→class DeckConfig
24→/**
25→ * Creates a copy from [JSONObject] and use it as a string
26→ *
27→ * This function will perform deepCopy on the passed object
28→ *
29→ */(json: JSONObject) : JSONObject() {
30→
31→    var conf: Long
32→        get() = getLong("conf")
33→        set(value) {
34→            put("conf", value)
35→        }
36→
37→    var id: DeckConfigId
38→        get() = getLong("id")
39→        set(value) {
40→            put("id", value)
41→        }
42→
43→    var name: String
44→        get() = getString("name")
45→        set(value) {
46→            put("name", value)
47→        }
48→
49→    companion object {
50→        fun parseTimer(config: JSONObject): Boolean? {
51→            // Note: Card.py used != 0, DeckOptions used == 1
52→            return try {
53→                // #6089 - Anki 2.1.24 changed this to a bool, reverted in 2.1.25.
54→                config.getInt("timer") != 0
55→            } catch (e: Exception) {
56→                Timber.w(e)
57→                try {
58→                    config.getBoolean("timer")
59→                } catch (ex: Exception) {
60→                    Timber.w(ex)
61→                    null
62→                }
63→            }
64→        }
65→
66→        /**
67→         * @return The 'timer' property on [config], or [defaultValue] if it's not set.
68→         */
69→        fun parseTimerOpt(config: JSONObject, defaultValue: Boolean): Boolean =
70→            parseTimer(config) ?: defaultValue
71→    }
72→
73→    init {
74→        json.deepClonedInto(this)
75→    }
76→}

16→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
17→
18→import android.content.Context
19→import android.content.Intent
20→import android.os.Bundle
21→import android.webkit.WebView
22→import androidx.activity.OnBackPressedCallback
23→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentActivity
24→import anki.collection.OpChanges
25→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
26→import com.ichi2.anki.OnPageFinishedCallback
27→import com.ichi2.anki.R
28→import com.ichi2.anki.SingleFragmentActivity
29→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
30→import com.ichi2.libanki.undoableOp
31→import com.ichi2.libanki.updateDeckConfigsRaw
32→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
33→import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
34→import timber.log.Timber
35→
36→@NeedsTest("pressing back: icon + button should go to the previous screen")
37→@NeedsTest("15130: pressing back: icon + button should return to options if the manual is open")
38→class DeckOptions : PageFragment() {
39→    override val title: String
40→        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu__deck_options)
41→    override val pageName = "deck-options"
42→    override lateinit var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
43→    override var webChromeClient = PageChromeClient()
44→
45→    // handle going back from the manual
46→    private val onBackCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
47→        override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
48→            Timber.v("webView: navigating back")
49→            webView.goBack()
50→        }
51→    }
52→
53→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
54→        val deckId = arguments?.getLong(ARG_DECK_ID)
55→            ?: throw Exception("missing deck ID")
56→        webViewClient = DeckOptionsWebClient(deckId)
57→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
58→        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, onBackCallback)
59→        webViewClient.onPageFinishedCallback = OnPageFinishedCallback { view ->
60→            Timber.v("canGoBack: %b", view.canGoBack())
61→            onBackCallback.isEnabled = view.canGoBack()
62→        }
63→    }
64→
65→    class DeckOptionsWebClient(val deckId: Long) : PageWebViewClient() {
66→        override val promiseToWaitFor: String
67→            get() = "\$deckOptions"
68→
69→        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
70→            // from upstream: https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/678c354fed4d98c0a8ef84fb7981ee085bd744a7/qt/aqt/deckoptions.py#L55
71→            view!!.evaluateJavascript("const \$deckOptions = anki.setupDeckOptions($deckId);") {
72→                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
73→            }
74→        }
75→    }
76→
77→    companion object {
78→        const val ARG_DECK_ID = "deckId"
79→
80→        fun getIntent(context: Context, deckId: Long): Intent {
81→            val arguments = Bundle().apply {
82→                putLong(ARG_DECK_ID, deckId)
83→            }
84→            return SingleFragmentActivity.getIntent(context, DeckOptions::class, arguments)
85→        }
86→    }
87→}
88→
89→suspend fun FragmentActivity.updateDeckConfigsRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
90→    val output = CollectionManager.withCol { updateDeckConfigsRaw(input) }
91→    undoableOp { OpChanges.parseFrom(output) }
92→    withContext(Dispatchers.Main) { finish() }
93→    return output
94→}

 53→// Here we extract per-module "best practices" settings to a single top-level evaluation
 54→subprojects {

 57→    afterEvaluate { project ->

 76→                systemProperties['junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.default'] = "concurrent"

 80→        /**

 82→         For those to work when Kotlin compilation targets the JVM backend, you have to enable the interoperability via
 83→         'freeCompilerArgs' in your gradle file, and you have to choose one of the appropriate '-Xjvm-default' modes.
 84→
 85→         https://kotlinlang.org/docs/java-to-kotlin-interop.html#default-methods-in-interfaces
 86→
 87→         and we used "all" because we don't have downstream consumers

 91→            coroutines API, this targets all project modules with the exception of the "api" module,

102→                }

109→ext {
110→
111→    jvmVersion = Jvm.current().javaVersion.majorVersion
112→    if (jvmVersion != "17" && jvmVersion != "21") {
113→        println "\n\n\n"
114→        println "**************************************************************************************************************"
115→        println "\n\n\n"
116→        println "ERROR: AnkiDroid builds with JVM version 17 or 21."
117→        println "  Incompatible major version detected: '" + jvmVersion + "'"
118→        println "\n\n\n"
119→        println "  If you receive this error because you want to use a newer JDK, we may accept PRs to support new versions."
120→        println "  Edit the main build.gradle file, find this message in the file, and add support for the new version."
121→        println "  Please make sure the `jacocoTestReport` target works on an emulator with our minSdkVersion (currently 23)."
122→        println "\n\n\n"
123→        println "**************************************************************************************************************"
124→        println "\n\n\n"
125→        System.exit(1)
126→    }
127→    ciBuild = System.getenv("CI") == "true" // works for Travis CI or Github Actions
128→    // allows for -Dpre-dex=false to be set
129→    preDexEnabled = "true" == System.getProperty("pre-dex", "true")
130→    // allows for universal APKs to be generated
131→    universalApkEnabled = "true" == System.getProperty("universal-apk", "false")
132→
133→    if (System.getProperty("os.name") == "Mac OS X") {
134→        // macOS reports hardware cores. This is accurate for CI, Intel (halved due to SMT) and Apple Silicon
135→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = "sysctl -n hw.physicalcpu".execute().text.toInteger()
136→    } else if (ciBuild) {
137→        // GitHub Actions run on Standard_D4ads_v5 Azure Compute Units with 4 vCPUs
138→        // They appear to be 2:1 vCPU to CPU on Linux/Windows with two vCPU cores but with performance 1:1-similar
139→        // Sources to determine the correct Azure Compute Unit (and get CPU count) to tune this:
140→        // Which Azure compute unit in use? https://github.com/github/docs/blob/a25a33bb6cbf86a629d0a0c7bef624743991f97e/content/actions/using-github-hosted-runners/about-github-hosted-runners/about-github-hosted-runners.md?plain=1#L176
141→        // What is that compute unit? https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/dasv5-dadsv5-series#dadsv5-series
142→        // How does it perform? https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dadsv5 (vs previous Standard_DS2_v2 https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dv2---general-compute)
143→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = 4
144→    } else {
145→        // Use 50% of cores to account for SMT which doesn't help this workload
146→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = Runtime.runtime.availableProcessors().intdiv(2) ?: 1
147→    }
148→}

 55→        // The version number is of the form:
 56→        // <major>.<minor>.<maintenance>[dev|alpha<build>|beta<build>|]
 57→        // The <build> is only present for alpha and beta releases (e.g., 2.0.4alpha2 or 2.0.4beta4), developer builds do
 58→        // not have a build number (e.g., 2.0.4dev) and official releases only have three components (e.g., 2.0.4).
 59→        //
 60→        // The version code is derived from the version name as follows:
 61→        // AbbCCtDD
 62→        // A: 1-digit decimal number representing the major version
 63→        // bb: 2-digit decimal number representing the minor version
 64→        // CC: 2-digit decimal number representing the maintenance version
 65→        // t: 1-digit decimal number representing the type of the build
 66→        // 0: developer build
 67→        // 1: alpha release
 68→        // 2: beta release
 69→        // 3: public release
 70→        // DD: 2-digit decimal number representing the build
 71→        // 00 for internal builds and public releases
 72→        // alpha/beta build number for alpha/beta releases
 73→        //
 74→        // This ensures the correct ordering between the various types of releases (dev < alpha < beta < release) which is
 75→        // needed for upgrades to be offered correctly.
 76→        versionCode=21800105
 77→        versionName="2.18alpha5"
 78→        minSdk 23 // also in testlib/build.gradle.kts

153→    /**
154→     * Product Flavors are used for Amazon App Store and Google Play Store.
155→     * This is because we cannot use Camera Permissions in Amazon App Store (for FireTv etc...)
156→     * Therefore, different AndroidManifest for Camera Permissions is used in Amazon flavor.

158→     * This flavor block must stay in sync with the same block in testlib/build.gradle.kts
159→     */
160→    flavorDimensions += "appStore"

164→        }
165→        create('amazon') {
166→            dimension "appStore"
167→        }

169→        create('full') {

171→        }
172→    }
173→
174→    /**
175→     * Set this to true to create five separate APKs instead of one:
176→     *   - 2 APKs that only work on ARM/ARM64 devices
177→     *   - 2 APKs that only works on x86/x86_64 devices
178→     *   - a universal APK that works on all devices
179→     * The advantage is the size of most APKs is reduced by about 2.5MB.
180→     * Upload all the APKs to the Play Store and people will download
181→     * the correct one based on the CPU architecture of their device.
182→     */
183→    def enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture = true
184→
185→    splits {
186→        abi {
187→            reset()
188→            enable enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture
189→            //universalApk enableUniversalApk  // set in debug + release config blocks above
190→            include "armeabi-v7a", "x86", "arm64-v8a", "x86_64"
191→        }
192→    }
193→    // applicationVariants are e.g. debug, release
194→    applicationVariants.configureEach { variant ->
195→        // We want the same version stream for all ABIs in debug but for release we can split them
196→        if (variant.buildType.name == 'release') {
197→            variant.outputs.configureEach { output ->
198→
199→                // For each separate APK per architecture, set a unique version code as described here:
200→                // https://developer.android.com/studio/build/configure-apk-splits.html
201→                def versionCodes = ["armeabi-v7a": 1, "x86": 2, "arm64-v8a": 3, "x86_64": 4]
202→                def outputFile = output.outputFile
203→                if (outputFile != null && outputFile.name.endsWith('.apk')) {
204→                    def abi = output.getFilter("ABI")
205→                    if (abi != null) {  // null for the universal-debug, universal-release variants
206→                        //  From: https://developer.android.com/studio/publish/versioning#appversioning
207→                        //  "Warning: The greatest value Google Play allows for versionCode is 2100000000"
208→                        //  AnkiDroid versionCodes have a budget 8 digits (through AnkiDroid 9)
209→                        //  This style does ABI version code ranges with the 9th digit as 0-4.
210→                        //  This consumes ~20% of the version range space, w/50 years of versioning at our major-version pace
211→                        output.versionCodeOverride =
212→                                // ex:  321200106 = 3 * 100000000 + 21200106
213→                                versionCodes.get(abi) * 100000000 + defaultConfig.versionCode
214→                    }
215→                }
216→            }
217→        }
218→    }

260→// Issue 11078 - some emulators run, but run zero tests, and still report success
261→tasks.register('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') {
262→    doLast {
263→        // androidTest currently creates one .xml file per emulator with aggregate results in this dir
264→        File folder = file("./build/outputs/androidTest-results/connected/flavors/play")
265→        File[] listOfFiles = folder.listFiles({ d, f -> f ==~ /.*.xml/ } as FilenameFilter)
266→        for (File file : listOfFiles) {
267→            // The aggregate results file currently contains a line with this pattern holding test count
268→            String[] matches = file.readLines().findAll { it.contains('<testsuite') }
269→            if (matches.length != 1) {
270→                throw new GradleScriptException("Unable to determine count of tests executed for " + file.name + ". Regex pattern out of date?", null)
271→            }
272→            if (!(matches[0] ==~ /.* tests="\d+" .*/) || matches[0].contains('tests="0"')) {
273→                throw new GradleScriptException("androidTest executed 0 tests for " + file.name + " - Probably a bug with the emulator. Try another image.", null)
274→            }
275→        }
276→    }
277→}
278→afterEvaluate {
279→    tasks.named('connectedPlayDebugAndroidTest').configure { finalizedBy('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') }
280→}

274→            // but be prepared to go with all default if it's a dynamic deck
275→            if (col.decks.isDyn(selectedDid)) {
276→                return null
277→            }
278→
279→            val revOptions = col.decks.confForDid(selectedDid).getJSONObject("rev")
280→
281→            if (revOptions.optBoolean("useGeneralTimeoutSettings", true)) {
282→                // we want to use the general settings, no need for per-deck settings
283→                return null
284→            }
285→
286→            val useTimer = revOptions.optBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
287→            val waitQuestionSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
288→            val waitAnswerSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
289→            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, useTimer, waitQuestionSecond, waitAnswerSecond)
290→        }

292→        fun queryFromPreferences(preferences: SharedPreferences, action: AutomaticAnswerAction): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
293→            val prefUseTimer: Boolean = preferences.getBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
294→            val prefWaitQuestionSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
295→            val prefWaitAnswerSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
296→            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, prefUseTimer, prefWaitQuestionSecond, prefWaitAnswerSecond)
297→        }
298→
299→        fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
300→            // deck specific options take precedence over general (preference-based) options.
301→            // the action can only be set via preferences (but is stored in the collection).
302→            val action = getAction(col)
303→            return queryDeckSpecificOptions(action, col, col.decks.selected()) ?: queryFromPreferences(preferences, action)
304→        }

34→/**
35→ * A Card is the ultimate entity subject to review; it encapsulates the scheduling parameters (from which to derive
36→ * the next interval), the note it is derived from (from which field data is retrieved), its own ownership (which deck it
37→ * currently belongs to), and the retrieval of presentation elements (filled-in templates).
38→ *
39→ * Card presentation has two components: the question (front) side and the answer (back) side. The presentation of the
40→ * card is derived from the template of the card's Card Type. The Card Type is a component of the Note Type (see Models)
41→ * that this card is derived from.
42→ *
43→ * This class is responsible for:
44→ * - Storing and retrieving database entries that map to Cards in the Collection
45→ * - Providing the HTML representation of the Card's question and answer
46→ * - Recording the results of review (answer chosen, time taken, etc)
47→ *
48→ * It does not:
49→ * - Generate new cards (see Collection)
50→ * - Store the templates or the style sheet (see Models)
51→ *
52→ * Type: 0=new, 1=learning, 2=due
53→ * Queue: same as above, and:
54→ * -1=suspended, -2=user buried, -3=sched buried
55→ * Due is used differently for different queues.
56→ * - new queue: note id or random int
57→ * - rev queue: integer day
58→ * - lrn queue: integer timestamp
59→ */
60→open class Card : Cloneable {
61→    // Needed for tests
62→    var col: Collection
63→
64→    /**
65→     * Time in MS when timer was started
66→     */
67→    var timerStarted: Long
68→

76→    var did: DeckId = 0
77→    var ord = 0
78→    var mod: Long = 0
79→    var usn = 0
80→
81→    @get:CARD_TYPE
82→    @CARD_TYPE
83→    var type = 0
84→
85→    @get:CARD_QUEUE
86→    @CARD_QUEUE

27→/**
28→ * The options for playing sound for a given card
29→ *
30→ * @param replayQuestion deck option: "Skip question when replaying answer".
31→ * `true`: replay the question and the answer
32→ * `false`: only replay the answer
33→ * @param autoplay deck option: "Don't play audio automatically"
34→ */
35→class CardSoundConfig(val replayQuestion: Boolean, val autoplay: Boolean, val deckId: DeckId) {
36→    // PERF: technically, we can go further with options groups
37→    @CheckResult
38→    fun appliesTo(card: Card): Boolean = CardUtils.getDeckIdForCard(card) == deckId
39→
40→    companion object {
41→        @CheckResult
42→        fun create(collection: Collection, card: Card): CardSoundConfig {
43→            Timber.v("start loading SoundConfig")
44→            val deckConfig = collection.decks.confForDid(CardUtils.getDeckIdForCard(card))
45→
46→            val autoPlay = deckConfig.optBoolean("autoplay", false)
47→
48→            val replayQuestion: Boolean = deckConfig.optBoolean("replayq", true)
49→
50→            return CardSoundConfig(replayQuestion, autoPlay, card.did).apply {
51→                Timber.d("loaded SoundConfig: %s", this)
52→            }
53→        }
54→
55→        @CheckResult
56→        suspend fun create(card: Card): CardSoundConfig = withCol { create(this, card) }
57→    }
58→}

22→class Deck : JSONObject {
23→    /**

25→     *
26→     * This function will perform deepCopy on the passed object
27→     *
28→     * If you want to create a Deck without deepCopy
29→     * @see Deck.from
30→     */
31→    constructor(json: JSONObject) : super() {
32→        json.deepClonedInto(this)
33→    }
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Creates a deck object form a json string
37→     */
38→    constructor(json: String) : super(json)
39→
40→    /**
41→     * Creates a new empty deck object
42→     */
43→    private constructor() : super()
44→
45→    val isFiltered: Boolean
46→        get() = getInt("dyn") == Consts.DECK_DYN
47→
48→    val isNormal: Boolean
49→        get() = !isFiltered
50→
51→    var name: String
52→        get() = getString("name")
53→        set(value) {
54→            put("name", value)
55→        }
56→
57→    var collapsed: Boolean
58→        get() = getBoolean("collapsed")
59→        set(value) {
60→            put("collapsed", value)
61→        }
62→
63→    var id: DeckId
64→        get() = getLong("id")
65→        set(value) {
66→            put("id", value)
67→        }
68→
69→    var conf: Long
70→        get() {
71→            val value = optLong("conf")
72→            return if (value > 0) value else 1
73→        }
74→        set(value) {
75→            put("conf", value)
76→        }
77→}
